message.content: read the dockerfiles and see if you can find any that use the `# xxx` to document per-line inclusions?

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

content.text: Let me read the Dockerfiles to find ones with per-line inclusion comments.

content.text:
Two Dockerfiles use the backtick-comment pattern for per-line package documentation:

**1. `rbev-vessels/rbev-ubu-safety/Dockerfile`** — the most thorough example, with 26 packages each annotated with `` `# description` `` inline comments (lines 9-34).

**2. `rbev-vessels/rbev-sentry-debian-slim/Dockerfile`** — uses the same pattern for a smaller set (lines 11-17): `bash`, `dnsmasq`, `iputils-ping`, `dnsutils`, `procps`.

The pattern is a backtick-quoted shell comment on each `\`-continued line of a `RUN apt-get install`:
```dockerfile
    bash            `# Shell for sentry setup script`       \
    dnsmasq         `# DNS forwarding for enclave`          \
```

This exploits the fact that `` `# ...` `` is a valid shell no-op (backtick-quoted comment becomes an empty string in the shell evaluation), so it documents each package without affecting the build. The other Dockerfiles use regular `#` comment lines but not this inline per-line technique.
